narration: Planets don't orbit in perfect circles — they dance in ellipses.
visual_description: Hook visual: Planets don't orbit in perfect circles — they dance in ellipses.

narration: In the early 1600s, Johannes Kepler cracked the code of planetary movement.
visual_description: Context visual: In the early 1600s, Johannes Kepler cracked the code of planetary movement.
ai_image_prompt: Context visual: In the early 1600s, Johannes Kepler cracked the code of planetary movement.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Context visual: In the early 1600s, Johannes Kepler cracked the code of planetary movement.

narration: He discovered that planets sweep out equal areas in equal times.
visual_description: Escalation visual: He discovered that planets sweep out equal areas in equal times.
ai_image_prompt: Escalation visual: He discovered that planets sweep out equal areas in equal times.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Escalation visual: He discovered that planets sweep out equal areas in equal times.

narration: Imagine a line from the sun to a planet. It covers the same area in a given time, whether it’s close or far.
visual_description: Evidence visual: Imagine a line from the sun to a planet. It covers the same area in a given time, whether it’s close or far.
ai_image_prompt: Evidence visual: Imagine a line from the sun to a planet. It covers the same area in a given time, whether it’s close or far.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Evidence visual: Imagine a line from the sun to a planet. It covers the same area in a given time, whether it’s close or far.

narration: This means planets move faster when they're nearer to the sun — gravity really is a game changer!
